contents:
# USS San Antonio Returns to Norfolk After 4th Fleet Deployment

## Overview
The USS San Antonio completed an eight-and-a-half-month deployment with the U.S. 4th Fleet. It returned to its homeport in Norfolk, Virginia, supporting the Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group and 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit.[1]

## Details
First-in-class San Antonio-class amphibious transport dock ship USS San Antonio (LPD 17) remained involved. The deployment focused on maritime operations in the U.S. 4th Fleet area. No casualties reported. The ship remains part of the Iwo Jima ARG. Return marked end of extended sea time. Homeport remains Naval Station Norfolk.[1]

**Location**: Norfolk, Virginia, USA

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @WarshipCam, which covers naval warship photography vessel identification. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following naval warship photography vessel identification will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
